rag_rules: Use `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks for AGREE contracts in component implementations
Place `assign` statements in implementation annexes only when the left-hand side is an existing output feature or auxiliary variable
Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Avoid encoding AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless the model exposes explicit typed ports or constants
generic_rules: Use exact current-case names from the model index in AGREE expressions
Treat each annex agree block as scoped to its owning component declaration
For implementation contracts, use `assign` only when the left-hand side is an existing output feature or declared auxiliary variable
For component type contracts, place input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees only when concrete bounds, named constants, or visible relations are available
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `assume`, `guarantee`, `eq`, `const`, and `assign` syntax in AGREE annex blocks
For output dependency contracts, write guarantees that relate visible outputs to visible inputs
Use case-provided thresholds and legal visible feature names in type scope contracts
anti_patterns: Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope
Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless the model exposes explicit typed ports or constants
irrelevant_topics: Annex placement in component types vs implementations
AADL property names as AGREE variables
